randomNumber:
*> Requires FUNCTION RANDOM, CURRENT-DATE (for seeding) - COBOL 2002+
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 RAND-SEED           PIC 9(8). *> Or larger if needed by compiler
*> 01 RAND-FLOAT          PIC 9V9(18). *> V indicates implied decimal point
*> 01 RAND-INT            PIC 9(9) COMP. *> Or appropriate size
*> 01 MIN-VAL             PIC 9(9) VALUE 1.
*> 01 MAX-VAL             PIC 9(9) VALUE 100.
*> 01 RANGE-SIZE          PIC 9(9).
*> 01 RAND-MOD            PIC 9(9).
*> 01 CURRENT-DATETIME    PIC 9(16). *> YYYYMMDDHHMMSShh

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
GENERATE-RANDOM-NUMBERS.
    *> Seed the random number generator (using time is common)
    MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO CURRENT-DATETIME.
    *> Extract seconds + hundredths for a somewhat varying seed
    MOVE CURRENT-DATETIME(13:4) TO RAND-SEED. *> Adjust based on desired seed source
    *> Seed function may vary by compiler (e.g., some use CALL)
    COMPUTE RAND-SEED = FUNCTION RANDOM(RAND-SEED). *> Call once for seed

    *> Generate a random float (0.0 <= x < 1.0)
    COMPUTE RAND-FLOAT = FUNCTION RANDOM().
    DISPLAY "Random Float (0 <= x < 1): " RAND-FLOAT.

    *> Generate a random integer within a range [MIN-VAL, MAX-VAL]
    COMPUTE RANGE-SIZE = MAX-VAL - MIN-VAL + 1.
    COMPUTE RAND-MOD = FUNCTION MOD(FUNCTION RANDOM * 1000000000, RANGE-SIZE).
    COMPUTE RAND-INT = RAND-MOD + MIN-VAL.
    DISPLAY "Random Integer (" MIN-VAL "-" MAX-VAL "): " RAND-INT.

    STOP RUN.
reverseString:
*> Requires FUNCTION REVERSE (COBOL 2002+) or manual loop.
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 ORIGINAL-STRING     PIC X(20) VALUE "HELLO COBOL".
*> 01 REVERSED-STRING     PIC X(20).
*> 01 STR-LEN             PIC 99 COMP.
*> 01 I                  PIC 99 COMP.
*> 01 J                  PIC 99 COMP.

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
REVERSE-THE-STRING.
    *> Method 1: Using intrinsic function (if available)
    MOVE FUNCTION REVERSE(FUNCTION TRIM(ORIGINAL-STRING))
        TO REVERSED-STRING.
    DISPLAY "Original:  " FUNCTION TRIM(ORIGINAL-STRING).
    DISPLAY "Reversed (Intrinsic): " FUNCTION TRIM(REVERSED-STRING).

    *> Method 2: Manual loop using reference modification
    *> Requires knowing the actual length of the string content
    *> Assume ORIGINAL-STRING is filled and we find its length
    INSPECT FUNCTION TRIM(ORIGINAL-STRING TRAILING) TALLYING STR-LEN FOR CHARACTERS.
    *> Or use: COMPUTE STR-LEN = FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(ORIGINAL-STRING)).

    MOVE SPACES TO REVERSED-STRING. *> Clear target
    MOVE 1 TO J.
    PERFORM VARYING I FROM STR-LEN BY -1 UNTIL I < 1
        MOVE ORIGINAL-STRING(I:1) TO REVERSED-STRING(J:1)
        ADD 1 TO J
    END-PERFORM.
    DISPLAY "Reversed (Manual Loop): " FUNCTION TRIM(REVERSED-STRING).

    STOP RUN.
fibonacci:
*> Calculate Nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed)
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 N                  PIC 9(3) VALUE 10. *> Term to calculate
*> 01 FIB-RESULT         PIC 9(18) COMP. *> Use large enough PIC, COMP recommended
*> 01 I                  PIC 9(3) COMP.
*> 01 A                  PIC S9(18) COMP VALUE 0. *> S for sign, needed for temp
*> 01 B                  PIC S9(18) COMP VALUE 1.
*> 01 NEXT-FIB           PIC S9(18) COMP.

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
CALCULATE-FIBONACCI.
    IF N < 0 THEN
        DISPLAY "Fibonacci input must be non-negative."
        STOP RUN
    END-IF.

    IF N = 0 THEN
        MOVE 0 TO FIB-RESULT
    ELSE IF N = 1 THEN
        MOVE 1 TO FIB-RESULT
    ELSE
        MOVE 0 TO A
        MOVE 1 TO B
        PERFORM VARYING I FROM 2 BY 1 UNTIL I > N
            COMPUTE NEXT-FIB = A + B
            MOVE B TO A
            MOVE NEXT-FIB TO B
        END-PERFORM
        MOVE B TO FIB-RESULT
    END-IF.

    DISPLAY "Fibonacci number F(" N ") is: " FIB-RESULT.

    STOP RUN.
factorial:
*> Calculate Factorial N!
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 N                  PIC 9(3) VALUE 5. *> Number for factorial
*> 01 FACT-RESULT        PIC 9(18) COMP VALUE 1. *> Max standard COBOL integer is often 18 digits
*> 01 I                  PIC 9(3) COMP.

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
CALCULATE-FACTORIAL.
    IF N < 0 THEN
        DISPLAY "Factorial input must be non-negative."
        STOP RUN
    END-IF.

    IF N > 20 THEN *> Factorial 21+ overflows PIC 9(18) / 64-bit
        DISPLAY "Input N > 20 may cause overflow."
        *> Could stop run or just proceed with likely incorrect result
    END-IF.

    MOVE 1 TO FACT-RESULT. *> Factorial of 0 is 1

    IF N > 0 THEN
        PERFORM VARYING I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL I > N
            COMPUTE FACT-RESULT = FACT-RESULT * I
        END-PERFORM
    END-IF.

    DISPLAY "Factorial of " N " is: " FACT-RESULT.

    STOP RUN.
primeCheck:
*> Check if a number is prime
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 NUM-TO-CHECK       PIC 9(18) VALUE 29.
*> 01 IS-PRIME-FLAG      PIC X(1) VALUE 'Y'. *> Y=Yes, N=No
*> 01 I                  PIC 9(18) COMP.
*> 01 LIMIT              PIC 9(9) COMP. *> Approx sqrt
*> 01 REMAINDER-CHECK    PIC 9(18).
*> 01 CHECK-RESULT       PIC 9(18).

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
CHECK-IF-PRIME.
    MOVE 'Y' TO IS-PRIME-FLAG. *> Assume prime initially

    IF NUM-TO-CHECK <= 1 THEN
        MOVE 'N' TO IS-PRIME-FLAG
    ELSE IF NUM-TO-CHECK <= 3 THEN
        MOVE 'Y' TO IS-PRIME-FLAG *> 2 and 3 are prime
    ELSE IF FUNCTION MOD(NUM-TO-CHECK, 2) = 0 OR
            FUNCTION MOD(NUM-TO-CHECK, 3) = 0 THEN
        MOVE 'N' TO IS-PRIME-FLAG *> Divisible by 2 or 3
    ELSE
        *> Check divisors from 5 upwards, stepping by 6
        COMPUTE LIMIT = FUNCTION SQRT(NUM-TO-CHECK)
        MOVE 5 TO I
        PERFORM UNTIL I > LIMIT
            DIVIDE NUM-TO-CHECK BY I
                GIVING CHECK-RESULT REMAINDER REMAINDER-CHECK
            END-DIVIDE
            IF REMAINDER-CHECK = 0 THEN
                MOVE 'N' TO IS-PRIME-FLAG
                EXIT PERFORM *> Exit loop early if divisor found
            END-IF

            ADD 2 TO I *> Check I+2
             DIVIDE NUM-TO-CHECK BY I
                GIVING CHECK-RESULT REMAINDER REMAINDER-CHECK
            END-DIVIDE
             IF REMAINDER-CHECK = 0 THEN
                MOVE 'N' TO IS-PRIME-FLAG
                EXIT PERFORM *> Exit loop early
            END-IF

            SUBTRACT 2 FROM I *> Go back to original I for next step
            ADD 6 TO I      *> Next step is I + 6
        END-PERFORM
    END-IF.

    *> Display result
    IF IS-PRIME-FLAG = 'Y' THEN
        DISPLAY NUM-TO-CHECK " is prime."
    ELSE
        DISPLAY NUM-TO-CHECK " is not prime."
    END-IF.

    STOP RUN.
readFile:
*> Read lines from a sequential file.
*> NOTE: Requires full ENVIRONMENT and DATA division setup not shown here.
*> Needs FILE-CONTROL entry mapping logical name to physical file.
*> Needs FD entry in FILE SECTION describing the file record layout.
*> Needs record structure (e.g., 01 FILE-RECORD PIC X(80)) in FILE SECTION.
*> Needs WORKING-STORAGE variable for EOF flag.

*> Example Skeleton:
*> FILE-CONTROL.
*>     SELECT INPUT-FILE ASSIGN TO "mydata.txt"
*>         ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
*>         FILE STATUS IS FILE-STATUS-CODE. *> Optional status code var

*> DATA DIVISION.
*> FILE SECTION.
*> FD INPUT-FILE.
*> 01 FILE-RECORD         PIC X(80).
*> WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
*> 01 WS-EOF-FLAG         PIC X(1) VALUE 'N'.
*>    88 EOF-REACHED             VALUE 'Y'.
*> 01 WS-LINE-COUNT        PIC 9(5) VALUE 0.
*> 01 FILE-STATUS-CODE    PIC XX. *> If FILE STATUS used

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
READ-FILE-LOGIC.
    OPEN INPUT INPUT-FILE.
    *> Check FILE STATUS code here if used (e.g., '00' is OK)

    PERFORM READ-NEXT-RECORD UNTIL EOF-REACHED.

    CLOSE INPUT-FILE.
    DISPLAY "Finished reading file. Lines read: " WS-LINE-COUNT.
    STOP RUN.

READ-NEXT-RECORD.
    READ INPUT-FILE
        AT END
            SET EOF-REACHED TO TRUE
        NOT AT END
            ADD 1 TO WS-LINE-COUNT
            DISPLAY "Line " WS-LINE-COUNT ": " FILE-RECORD
            *> Process FILE-RECORD here
    END-READ.
writeFile:
*> Write lines to a sequential file.
*> NOTE: Requires full ENVIRONMENT and DATA division setup similar to readFile.
*> Needs FILE-CONTROL entry mapping logical name to physical file.
*> Needs FD entry in FILE SECTION describing the file record layout.
*> Needs record structure (e.g., 01 OUTPUT-RECORD PIC X(80)) in FILE SECTION.
*> Needs WORKING-STORAGE variables for data to write.

*> Example Skeleton:
*> FILE-CONTROL.
*>     SELECT OUTPUT-FILE ASSIGN TO "output_cobol.txt"
*>         ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.

*> DATA DIVISION.
*> FILE SECTION.
*> FD OUTPUT-FILE.
*> 01 OUTPUT-RECORD       PIC X(80).
*> WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
*> 01 LINE-TO-WRITE-1     PIC X(80) VALUE "First line written by COBOL.".
*> 01 LINE-TO-WRITE-2     PIC X(80) VALUE "Second line.".

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
WRITE-FILE-LOGIC.
    OPEN OUTPUT OUTPUT-FILE. *> Opens for write (overwrite)
    *> To append use: OPEN EXTEND OUTPUT-FILE.

    *> Move data to the file record buffer and write
    MOVE LINE-TO-WRITE-1 TO OUTPUT-RECORD.
    WRITE OUTPUT-RECORD.

    MOVE LINE-TO-WRITE-2 TO OUTPUT-RECORD.
    WRITE OUTPUT-RECORD.

    CLOSE OUTPUT-FILE.
    DISPLAY "Finished writing to file.".
    STOP RUN.
fileExists:
*> Checking file existence in standard COBOL is tricky and often
*> depends on checking FILE STATUS after attempting to OPEN the file.
*> There isn't a simple intrinsic function like in other languages.
*> This shows the concept using OPEN INPUT and checking status.

*> Requires FILE-CONTROL entry like:
*> SELECT CHECK-FILE ASSIGN TO DYNAMIC FILE-TO-CHECK-NAME
*>     FILE STATUS IS FILE-CHECK-STATUS.

*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 FILE-TO-CHECK-NAME PIC X(60) VALUE "mydata.txt". *> Or get dynamically
*> 01 FILE-CHECK-STATUS  PIC XX.
*>    88 FILE-OK                  VALUE "00". *> Successful open
*>    88 FILE-NOT-FOUND           VALUE "35". *> Common code for Not Found
*>    *> Other status codes exist for permissions, etc.

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
CHECK-FILE-EXISTENCE.
    OPEN INPUT CHECK-FILE. *> Attempt to open for reading

    IF FILE-OK THEN
        DISPLAY "File '" FUNCTION TRIM(FILE-TO-CHECK-NAME) "' exists."
        *> MUST close it if successfully opened for check
        CLOSE CHECK-FILE
    ELSE IF FILE-NOT-FOUND THEN
        DISPLAY "File '" FUNCTION TRIM(FILE-TO-CHECK-NAME) "' not found."
    ELSE *> Other error
        DISPLAY "Cannot determine status or other error for file '"
                 FUNCTION TRIM(FILE-TO-CHECK-NAME) "'."
        DISPLAY "File Status Code: " FILE-CHECK-STATUS
        *> May need to CLOSE CHECK-FILE here too depending on error
    END-IF.

    STOP RUN.
dateTime:
*> Get current date and time using intrinsic function.
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE entries like:
*> 01 WS-CURRENT-DATETIME.
*>    05 WS-CURR-YEAR     PIC 9(4).
*>    05 WS-CURR-MONTH    PIC 9(2).
*>    05 WS-CURR-DAY      PIC 9(2).
*>    05 WS-CURR-HOUR     PIC 9(2).
*>    05 WS-CURR-MINUTE   PIC 9(2).
*>    05 WS-CURR-SECOND   PIC 9(2).
*>    05 WS-CURR-HUNDREDTH PIC 9(2). *> Hundredths of a second
*>    05 WS-GMT-OFFSET    PIC S9(4) SIGN LEADING SEPARATE. *> +/-hhmm
*> 01 DATE-TIME-STR       PIC X(21). *> To receive full CURRENT-DATE result

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
GET-DATE-TIME.
    *> CURRENT-DATE returns YYYYMMDDHHMMSShh[+/-]hhmm (21 bytes)
    MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO DATE-TIME-STR.

    *> Extract components using reference modification
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(1:4)  TO WS-CURR-YEAR.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(5:2)  TO WS-CURR-MONTH.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(7:2)  TO WS-CURR-DAY.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(9:2)  TO WS-CURR-HOUR.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(11:2) TO WS-CURR-MINUTE.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(13:2) TO WS-CURR-SECOND.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(15:2) TO WS-CURR-HUNDREDTH.
    MOVE DATE-TIME-STR(17:5) TO WS-GMT-OFFSET. *> Includes sign

    DISPLAY "Current Date/Time Information:".
    DISPLAY "Full String: " DATE-TIME-STR.
    DISPLAY "Year:   " WS-CURR-YEAR.
    DISPLAY "Month:  " WS-CURR-MONTH.
    DISPLAY "Day:    " WS-CURR-DAY.
    DISPLAY "Hour:   " WS-CURR-HOUR.
    DISPLAY "Minute: " WS-CURR-MINUTE.
    DISPLAY "Second: " WS-CURR-SECOND.
    DISPLAY "Hundredths: " WS-CURR-HUNDREDTH.
    DISPLAY "GMT Offset: " WS-GMT-OFFSET.

    *> Note: Getting Unix Epoch seconds requires more complex date/time
    *> calculations or potentially system calls/compiler extensions.

    STOP RUN.
maxInArray:
*> Find the maximum value in a COBOL table (array).
*> Requires WORKING-STORAGE setup for the table and variables.

*> Example Skeleton:
*> DATA DIVISION.
*> WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
*> 01 NUMBER-TABLE.
*>    05 NUM-ITEM        PIC S9(9) COMP OCCURS 10 TIMES
*>                       INDEXED BY IDX. *> Indexed for PERFORM VARYING
*> 01 TABLE-SIZE         PIC 99 COMP VALUE 10. *> Number of items defined
*> 01 ITEMS-LOADED       PIC 99 COMP VALUE 0. *> Actual items used (if variable)
*> 01 MAX-VALUE          PIC S9(9) COMP.
*> 01 I                  PIC 99 COMP. *> Loop counter if not using index

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
FIND-MAX-VALUE.
    *> Assume NUMBER-TABLE is populated somehow, e.g.:
    MOVE 10 TO NUM-ITEM(1).
    MOVE 5 TO NUM-ITEM(2).
    MOVE 42 TO NUM-ITEM(3).
    MOVE 17 TO NUM-ITEM(4).
    MOVE 8 TO NUM-ITEM(5).
    MOVE 99 TO NUM-ITEM(6).
    MOVE 32 TO NUM-ITEM(7).
    MOVE -5 TO NUM-ITEM(8).
    MOVE 0 TO NUM-ITEM(9).
    MOVE 20 TO NUM-ITEM(10).
    MOVE 10 TO ITEMS-LOADED. *> Set actual number of items

    IF ITEMS-LOADED = 0 THEN
        DISPLAY "Table is empty, cannot find maximum."
        STOP RUN
    END-IF.

    *> Initialize MAX-VALUE with the first element
    MOVE NUM-ITEM(1) TO MAX-VALUE.

    *> Loop through the rest of the table using index
    PERFORM VARYING IDX FROM 2 BY 1 UNTIL IDX > ITEMS-LOADED
        IF NUM-ITEM(IDX) > MAX-VALUE THEN
            MOVE NUM-ITEM(IDX) TO MAX-VALUE
        END-IF
    END-PERFORM.

    DISPLAY "The maximum value in the table is: " MAX-VALUE.

    STOP RUN.
